purpose: Define the minimal governance-facing root kernel every independent service should expose while leaving implementation layout flexible.

notes: app and src are alternatives; at least one is typically expected.
The governance kernel does not force a packaging strategy.
ops is recommended when release handling is queue or batch oriented.

rules: The governance kernel is mandatory for independently linked services.
Implementation folders outside the governance kernel are not centrally fixed.
A linked service may be marked planned before the kernel is fully present, but the contract path must still be declared.
The governance-facing root must embed the DTP surface under governance/dtp using the fixed trace, journal index, and validator receipt paths.
Temporary-link kits must live under governance/links/active while evidence and review payloads remain in governance/evidence and governance/reviews.
Optional runtime entries remain service-local and are never mandatory central kernel requirements.
